revision.comment: 新しいページ：豊岡上天神社。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = 式内社（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = [[9月18日]]（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平）|1983年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気姫命]]（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、[[岡豊城]]築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1088313/514 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（近代デジタルライブラリーより）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[日本歴史地名体系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=[[山本大]]|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:南国市]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]]

社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）

{{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = [[9月18日]]（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気姫命]]（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、[[岡豊城]]築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1088313/514 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（近代デジタルライブラリーより）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=[[山本大]]|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = [[9月18日]]（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気姫命]]（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、[[岡豊城]]築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=[[山本大]]|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]]

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

[[JA:豊岡上天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119690}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|豊岡上天神社|version=107359344|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = [[9月18日]]（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=豊岡
上天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気姫命]]（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、[[岡豊城]]築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=山本大|authorlink=山本大|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

(元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == 社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = [[9月18日]]（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=豊岡
上天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気姫命]]（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、[[岡豊城]]築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** 第9巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] {{wikidata link|Q22119690}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|豊岡上天神社|version=107359344|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = [[9月18日]]（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=豊岡
上天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町常通寺島にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * [[トヨウケビメ|屋船豊宇気姫命]] 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[岡豊城]]のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気姫命]]（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、[[岡豊城]]築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[土佐国]][[長岡郡]]に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『[[土佐物語]]』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした[[長宗我部氏]]の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 == 摂末社 == * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=山本大|authorlink=山本大|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] {{nihongo|'''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha'''|豊岡上天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]].

Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3490005004886 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町常通寺島618', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:豊岡上天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119690}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|南国市|Q861621}}岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月18日|Q2893}}（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=豊岡
上天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、{{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|南国市|Q861621}}岡豊町常通寺島にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊宇気姫命}}（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|土佐国|Q857330}}{{jalink|長岡郡|Q1132123}}に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『{{jalink|土佐物語|Q11423040}}』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした{{jalink|長宗我部氏|Q981303}}の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 === 摂末社 === * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 40 高知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=山本大|authorlink=山本大|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3490005004886 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町常通寺島618', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:豊岡上天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119690}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|南国市|Q861621}}岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月18日|Q2893}}（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=豊岡
上天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、{{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|南国市|Q861621}}岡豊町常通寺島にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊宇気姫命}}（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|土佐国|Q857330}}{{jalink|長岡郡|Q1132123}}に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『{{jalink|土佐物語|Q11423040}}』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした{{jalink|長宗我部氏|Q981303}}の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 === 摂末社 === * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 40 高知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=山本大|authorlink=山本大|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] 豊岡上天神社 |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|南国市|Q861621}}岡豊町常通寺島618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=豊岡上天神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月18日|Q2893}}（諸説あり）{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=豊岡
上天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''豊岡上天神社'''（とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}/とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}）は、{{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|南国市|Q861621}}岡豊町常通寺島にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊宇気姫命}}（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|土佐国|Q857330}}{{jalink|長岡郡|Q1132123}}に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『{{jalink|土佐物語|Q11423040}}』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした{{jalink|長宗我部氏|Q981303}}の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 === 摂末社 === * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 40 高知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=山本大|authorlink=山本大|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka {{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山の西南麓に鎮座する。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|屋船豊宇気姫命}} 『土佐国式社考』では、社名「豊岡」と神名「豊宇気姫（豊宇賀能売）」の関連が指摘されている{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}のある岡豊山]] 由緒は不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。当地の開拓にあたり農業神の{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊宇気姫命}}（豊受姫命）を奉斎したことに始まるといわれる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。元々は岡豊山の山上にあったが、{{jalink|岡豊城|Q11048926}}築城にあたり移されたとも伝わる{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|土佐国|Q857330}}{{jalink|長岡郡|Q1132123}}に「豊岡上天神社」と見え、これは当社に比定される{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。社名の「豊岡」に関しては地名「岡豊」の古称とする説や（『{{jalink|土佐物語|Q11423040}}』）、長岡郡の豊かな岡を意味するとする説、土佐国府に近いため「お国府（こふ）」に由来するとする説等があるが不詳{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。「天神」の由来についても諸説があり明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後は岡豊城を拠点とした{{jalink|長宗我部氏|Q981303}}の崇敬を受けたものと推測されるが、史料が無く明らかではない{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 === 摂末社 === * 若宮神社{{Sfn|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）514コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=豊岡上天神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 40 高知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=山本大|authorlink=山本大|chapter=豊岡上天神社|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊岡上天神社（式内社）|1987年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3490005004886 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町常通寺島618', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:豊岡上天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119690}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 18|Q2893}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}} ({{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{jalink|Nagaoka District|Q1132123}}, {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{jalink|Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{jalink|Chōsokabe clan|Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3490005004886 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町常通寺島618', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:豊岡上天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119690}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 18|Q2893}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}} ({{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{jalink|Nagaoka District|Q1132123}}, {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{jalink|Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{jalink|Chōsokabe clan|Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 18|Q2893}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}} ({{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{jalink|Nagaoka District|Q1132123}}, {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{jalink|Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{jalink|Chōsokabe clan|Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]]

Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{jalink|September 18|Q2893}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{jalink|Nankoku|Q861621}}, {{jalink|Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{jalink|Okō Castle|Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{jalink|927|Q30470}} ({{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{jalink|Nagaoka District|Q1132123}}, {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{jalink|Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{jalink|Chōsokabe clan|Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよおかかみてんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐国の式内社]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893|1=September 18}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040|1=Tosa Monogatari}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite
