Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893|1=September 18}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040|1=Tosa Monogatari}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040|1=Tosa Monogatari}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893|1=September 18}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040|1=Tosa Monogatari}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/514}} ''Meiji Shrine Materials, Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 514. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha |画像 = [[File:Toyooka-kamitenjinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893|1=September 18}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040|1=Tosa Monogatari}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha|title={{ill|lt=Prefectural and District Shrines: Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Compilation University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Jōtsūji-jima 618 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|32.53|N|133|37|10.44|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 18|Q2893|WD=Q2893|1=September 18}} (various theories){{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Toyooka
Kami Tenjinsha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha''' (とよおかかみてんじんじゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}} / とよおかかみてんじんしゃ{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōtsūji-jima, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It stands at the southwestern foot of Mt. Okō, where {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} is located. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Yafune Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} In ''Tosa-koku Shikishakō'', a relation is pointed out between the shrine name “Toyooka” and the deity name “Toyouke-hime (Toyouka-no-me)”{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === History === [[File:Oko castle zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Okō with {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}}]] Its history is unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is said to have begun when the agricultural deity {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto) was worshiped during development of the area{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It was originally on top of Mt. Okō, but is also said to have been moved when {{ill|lt=Okō Castle|Q11048926|WD=Q11048926|1=Okō Castle}} was built{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. As for “Toyooka,” theories include an old name for the place name “Okō” (''{{ill|lt=Tosa Monogatari|Q11423040|WD=Q11423040|1=Tosa Monogatari}}''), “a rich hill in Nagaoka District,” or derivation from “okokufu” because of proximity to the Tosa provincial office, but it is uncertain{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. The origin of “Tenjin” is also unclear, with multiple theories{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. It is inferred to have later been revered by the {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe clan|Q981303|WD=Q981303|1=Chōsokabe clan}}, based at Okō Castle, but there are no documents and details are unclear{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine{{Sfn|Toyooka Kami Tenjinsha
